title: Trace the reference count
prompt_md:
## Goal

A `std::shared_ptr` tracks how many owners share its object via a reference
count. Maintain a set of owners that grows and shrinks under a stream of
commands, and report the reference count after each command.

Start with one original `shared_ptr` (created with `std::make_shared`). Keep any
extra owners in a container. Then process `q` commands:

- `share` -- add one more owner (a copy of the shared pointer).
- `release` -- drop one of the extra owners, if any exist. The original owner is
  never dropped.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `q` (1 <= q <= 50), the number of commands.
- Next `q` lines: each is `share` or `release`.

## Output

- After each command, a line with the current `use_count()` of the original
  `shared_ptr`.

## Notes

A `release` when there are no extra owners leaves the count unchanged. No raw
`new`/`delete`.

test_cases.label: Growing then shrinking
test_cases.stdin:
6
share
share
share
release
release
release

title: Shared ownership ledger
prompt_md:
## Goal

A resource often starts under a single exclusive owner (a `std::unique_ptr`) and
is later **published** to many shared readers. Model two such resources, hand each
`unique_ptr`'s object to a `std::shared_ptr`, then run a stream of commands that
distribute and drop reader copies between them. Because the two control blocks
have **independent** reference counts that move under the command stream, the
printed `use_count`s can only be reproduced by actually holding, copying, and
destroying `shared_ptr`s -- a closed-form formula over the inputs cannot.

## Input

- Line 1: integers `va`, `vb`, and `q` (-100000 <= va, vb <= 100000;
  1 <= q <= 50): the two resource values and the number of commands.
- Next `q` lines, each one of:
  - `holdA` -- add a reader copy of resource A.
  - `holdB` -- add a reader copy of resource B.
  - `drop` -- drop the most recently added reader copy (if any).
  - `report` -- print the current owner counts of A and B.
  - `read` -- print the sum of the values seen through all current reader copies.

## Output

- For each `report`: `A=<a> B=<b>` where `<a>`/`<b>` are the `use_count()` of the
  original A / B `shared_ptr`.
- For each `read`: `sum=<s>` where `<s>` is the sum of `*reader` over every reader
  copy currently held (A's value for each A-copy, B's value for each B-copy).
- `holdA`, `holdB`, and `drop` produce no output.

## Notes

A `unique_ptr` cannot be copied, so each resource's ownership must be **moved**
into its `shared_ptr` (`std::shared_ptr<int> s{ std::move(u) };`). Keep reader
copies in a container; use `std::make_unique` / `std::make_shared`; no raw
`new`/`delete`, and no `weak_ptr`.

test_cases.label: Interleaved holds
test_cases.stdin:
10 20 7
holdA
holdB
report
holdA
report
read
drop

test_cases.label: Drop then read
test_cases.stdin:
7 3 6
holdA
holdA
drop
report
holdB
read
